what_it_does: The bridge plan that maps confirmed design-analysis findings to concrete changes in the real ConceptV design system, ordered by readiness and dependency (foundation tokens before container structure before template library), with a readiness key and a CAPSTONE section describing the unified build after all 12 ingest folders were analysed.
observations: States nothing is built yet — this is a plan that maps findings to changes.

Section 1 lists token recalibrations (zone ladder near-white values, soften gold #E0C010 to #d6bf57, warm bronze, add a 4-step brand ramp) with explicit deprecate-don't-rename and keep-the-color-mix-machinery guidance.
Section 2 adds new tokens: hatch, blueprint ghost, frame signature, two bullet semantics, status traffic-light, and 3:2 + web frame ratios.

Section 3 is the central structural build: a recursive typed-container library (Band/Section/Zone/Panel/Cluster/atoms) each with role + spacing rhythm + collapse rule + depth-keyed wash, serving decks AND the app from one model.
Sections 4-7 cover template library, axis controls, motion/space-time, and the deck-to-app bridge, each gated on the prior structural decision.
Suggests a concrete first execution slice (token recalibration, refresh zone specimen, then pause for the container-library decision).

CAPSTONE reduces the whole system to one type system + one rule engine + two layout solvers (block flow-stacking, graph relational) computed by axis-dials over an invariant DNA core, with a consolidated dependency-ordered build order and a doc index.
Uses a readiness legend: green ready now, yellow ready after a structural decision, blue confirm with more folders.
standouts: The capstone reduction: one type system + one rule engine + two layout solvers (block + graph) under axis-dials over an invariant DNA core.
One container model deliberately serving both presentation decks AND the live app (nav/panel/table/row is the same mutable tree).
Explicit dependency ordering: foundation (values) -> structure (containers) -> library (templates).
conventions: Readiness-emoji legend tied to evidence and risk.
Each section names a concrete WHERE (file or directory) for the change.
Deprecate-don't-rename / keep-old-as-legacy guidance to avoid breaking consumers.
Cross-references sibling analysis docs as the source of confirmed findings.
notes: The execution roadmap that turns the analysis corpus into a single generative core, foundation-first, with the container library called out as the deliberate keystone decision.
questions: Are the specific near-white hex targets here reconciled with the slightly different values quoted in REQUIREMENTS.md / recent-pitches.md?
Which product-UI-heavy ingest folder is intended to de-risk the deck-to-app bridge (section 7)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — titled a synthesis plan and it is a readiness-ordered, dependency-aware mapping from findings to concrete build steps.
apparent_themes: One generative core serving every surface (deck and app)
Deliberate structural decisions gated, not rushed
Single-source token recalibration with backward-compatible deprecation
intention_signals: Trying to sequence the move from analysis into a real build so the riskiest piece (the recursive container library) is a deliberate, well-supported decision, while the low-risk fidelity gains (tokens) land first.
novelty: The explicit reduction of the entire design system to one type system + one rule engine + two layout solvers (block and graph) under axis-dials, and using the same container tree for both decks and the app.
contribution: Contributes the readiness/dependency-ordered build sequence, the token-recalibration target values, the recursive container architecture, and the capstone unified-core formulation that a fused system would keep as its build map.
summary_for_embedding: A readiness- and dependency-ordered plan mapping confirmed design findings to concrete builds (token recalibration, a recursive typed-container library, template library, axis controls, motion, deck-to-app bridge) culminating in a unified generative core.
intention_for_embedding: To sequence the transition from design analysis into a real build so the unified generative core is constructed foundation-first with the container library as a deliberate keystone decision.
